governing_bodies.note: Historically gerrymandered — new maps in effect as of 2024. Need specific address for districts.

advocacy_orgs.name: Madison YIMBY
advocacy_orgs.alignment: Moderate — aligns on housing but this profile is less engaged on the issue

key_political_dynamics:
Madison is one of the most progressive cities in the Midwest, dominated by the UW-Madison community. Local politics often focus on education funding, policing reform, and environmental issues. Housing is an emerging issue as the city grows. Wisconsin state politics feature a Democratic governor (Evers) working against a historically gerrymandered (now redrawn) legislature. State-level education funding and Medicaid expansion are key ongoing fights.

June 4, 2026 updates: HOUSING OVERHAUL IN PROGRESS. Missing middle housing zoning overhaul approved — the most significant land use reform in years. PD rezoning outreach running April-June 2026. Housing Forward plan targets 15,000 units by 2030, with 25% affordable. 425 new rental units approved, 260+ permanently affordable.
enrichment_notes: Need specific address for alder district and state legislative districts. Madison uses Legistar — scraper should work.
